stories.title: Trump's deadline for an Iran deal looms. And, Artemis II crew begins the journey home
stories.description: In a press conference last night, Trump reiterated threats against Iran if the country doesn't accept a deal by 8:00 p.m. ET tonight. And, the Artemis II crew are on their way back to Earth.

stories.title: As Trump's deadline approaches, Iranian leaders respond in defiance
stories.description: Hours away from President Trump's 8 pm ET Tuesday deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, attacks continued in the Persian Gulf with no agreement in sight. Trump has threatened to bomb Iranian bridges and power plants if a deal is not reached.

stories.title: Beer cans, helium balloons and mortgages: An unexpected mix of things affected by war
stories.description: It's not just oil and gas that are affected by the Iran war. All sorts of shortages and price spikes are starting to pop up that stand to affect people's daily lives.

stories.title: Trump provides details behind the dramatic rescue of an American airman trapped in Iran
stories.description: President Trump revealed many of the dramatic details on how the U.S. military scrambled to rescue two members of fighter jet that was shot down deep inside Iran.

stories.title: Trump reiterates threats to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges
stories.description: The president has had mixed messages about how and when the U.S.-Israel-led war in Iran will end.

stories.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants, bridges. And, Artemis II readies for lunar flyby
stories.description: Trump threatened to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz. And, NASA's Artemis II crew prepares to make its closest approach to the moon.

stories.title: Iran rejects a U.S. ceasefire plan as Trump again threatens to bomb its infrastructure
stories.description: Iran's top officials pushed back against a U.S. ceasefire plan and President Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, striking a defiant tone as the warring sides traded missile attacks.

stories.title: Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
stories.description: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

stories.title: 'We're sinking deeper': Iranians brace for infrastructure strikes as Trump deadline nears
stories.description: Ordinary Iranians respond to the US president's threat to destroy Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if no deal before deadline
stories.description: The US president wants Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy route, before his Tuesday deadline.

stories.title: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
stories.description: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

stories.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
stories.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

stories.title: Trump declares victory after rescue but threats to US operation in Iran still loom
stories.description: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

stories.title: US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
stories.description: The niece and grand-niece of Qasem Soleimani are in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, officials said.

stories.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

stories.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
stories.description: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

stories.title: 'This has got me worried': Iranians fear what comes next after US strike on Karaj bridge
stories.description: President Trump has warned Iran of strikes on bridges and electric power plants if its leaders do not agree to his terms to end the war.

stories.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
stories.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

stories.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
stories.description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

stories.title: Iran's focus on survival means same regime still firmly in place
stories.description: Tehran is showing resilience despite Trump's claims of regime change, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

stories.title: When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines
stories.description: Experts say many US presidents have offered a timeline for a conflict - only to then shift their estimates.

stories.title: Ros Atkins on…the cost of the Iran war
stories.description: The BBC's Analysis Editor Ros Atkins takes a closer look at US costs as it continues its war with with Iran.

stories.title: NGO on Gaza: 'We don’t share the same definition of ceasefire'
stories.description: As another ten people have been killed in an airstrike in central Gaza, an NGO speaks to FRANCE 24 about the ongoing catastrophic situation for Palestinians, both in the Gaza Strip and the West Bank. The latest incident comes despite a fragile ceasefire put in place last October....

stories.title: Trump says can take out Iran 'in one night' as deadline ticks closer
stories.description: US President Donald Trump expanded his threat against Iran to include all power plants and bridges Monday as his ultimatum to make a deal ticked closer, after Tehran rejected a 45-day ceasefire proposal and said it wants a permanent end to the war. “The entire country can be take...

stories.title: Trump's Tuesday deadline: Will he really ‘bomb Iran into the Stone Age’?
stories.description: PRESS REVIEW - Tuesday, April 7: Donald Trump's blatantly disregards accusations of war crimes as he vows to bomb Iran's energy infrastructure if it does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Australia's most decorated military veteran is arrested and charged with alleged war crimes d...

stories.title: ‘Remarkable resilience’ in Iran as Trump deadline looms
stories.description: With just hours left to a deadline given to Iran by US President Donald Trump to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face massive strikes on infrastructure, there is tension on the streets on Tehran among ordinary Iranians, reports France 24’s correspondent Reza Sayah. But many have a...

stories.title: Trump's threatened destruction of Iranian infrastructure could be a war crime, legal experts say
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said Monday that he was “not at all” concerned about the risk of committing war crimes in Iran if he carried out his threat to bomb bridges and power plants. Experts in military law, however, say that deliberately bombing civilian infrastructure is a vio...

stories.title: Middle East war live: Iran warns of reprisals beyond the region if the US ‘crosses red lines’
stories.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards warned on state television Tuesday they would respond beyond the region and deprive the US and its allies of oil and gas "for years" if Washington crossed Tehran's "red lines". Their warning came after US President Donald Trump threatened to take out t...

stories.title: How US, Israel are waging a war on Iranian culture, education
stories.description: Iranian officials have told Al Jazeera the US and Israel's attacks seek to wipe out Iranian identity.

stories.title: Iranian missile strike damages cars and street in Israel
stories.description: Video shows damaged sidewalks and an overturned car following an Iranian missile strike on Ramat Hasharon in IsraeL.

stories.title: US-Israeli strikes destroy buildings in northern Iran
stories.description: Video shows widespread destruction in Pardis, northern Iran. The Israeli military said they bombed Iran on April 7.

stories.title: ‘Military action is not an effective means to pursue nonproliferation’
stories.description: 'We’re now seeing very concerning strikes on nuclear facilities in Iran that risk further radioactive contamination.'

stories.title: Trump and Hegseth invoke God and religion in Iran war press conference
stories.description: Trump believes God backs US in Iran war, Hegseth compares airman's rescue to Jesus' resurrection

stories.title: Iran releases on bail Japan national jailed amid antigovernment protests
stories.description: The person released is believed to be Japanese broadcaster NHK's Tehran bureau chief, Shinnosuke Kawashima.

stories.title: Synagogue in Tehran ‘completely destroyed’ in US-Israeli attack
stories.description: US-Israeli strikes destroy synagogue in Tehran, prompting outrage amid reports of 15 killed across Iran overnight.

stories.title: Lebanese firefighters respond to aftermath of Israeli strike
stories.description: Video shows Lebanese firefighters trying to put out flames in the southern city of Maarakeh.

stories.title: What is driving the Houthis’ decision-making on joining the Iran war
stories.description: There are two currents within the Houthis: one favouring caution, and another favouring commitment to allies.

stories.title: How Pakistan’s solar boom is shielding it from worst of Iran war crisis
stories.description: A quarter of Pakistani households are now using solar panels. This insulates millions of families from the energy crisis

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 39 of US-Israeli attacks?
stories.description: Trump has issued a strict deadline, but a defiant Iran says the threats by the US president are 'delusional'.

stories.title: Israel threatens Iran’s trains, railways before Trump’s deadline expires
stories.description: The bridge linking Saudi Arabia and Bahrain has been briefly closed as Trump threatens to hit Iranian infrastructure.

stories.title: Iran says US-Israeli projectile has struck a synagogue in Tehran
stories.description: A US-Israeli strike has caused extensive damage to a synagogue in Tehran, according to a video by Mehr News.

stories.title: Trump’s threat to strike Iran’s power plants looms
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said ‘Tuesday will be power plant day’ in a vulgar post on social media.

stories.title: ‘Dangerous escalation’: Pakistan condemns Iranian strikes on Saudi Arabia’s energy facilities
stories.description: Pakistan on Tuesday expressed concern and also condemnation of Iranian missile and drone strikes on Saudi Arabia’s energy facilities, terming the attacks as “a dangerous escalation” that undermined regional peace and stability. In a statement, the Foreign Office (FO) said, “The g...

stories.title: Pakistan condemns storming of Al Aqsa Mosque by Israeli occupation forces
stories.description: Pakistan on Tuesday strongly condemned the storming of Al Aqsa Mosque by Israeli occupation forces, the Foreign Office (FO) said. The reaction comes a day after far-right Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir stormed East Jerusalem’s Al Aqsa Mosque compound, accordin...

stories.title: UNSC to vote on Hormuz resolution ahead of Trump deadline
stories.description: WASHINGTON: The United Nations Security Council is set to vote on a Bahrain-drafted resolution on the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday, just hours before US President Donald Trump’s self-imposed deadline for Iran to reopen the strategic waterway. The resolution, drafted by Bahrain in ...

stories.title: Air India CEO Campbell Wilson resigns amid losses and regulatory scrutiny: report
stories.description: Air India Chief Executive Officer (CEO) Campbell Wilson has resigned, a source with direct knowledge of the matter said on Tuesday. The development comes as the airline grapples with persistent losses and heightened regulatory scrutiny following a crash last year that killed 260 ...

stories.title: Israeli strikes target Iran’s petrochemical facilities
stories.description: • Iran says ‘situation under control’; multiple airports bombed around capital• IRGC, Quds force officials assassinated; over 15 citizens killed in Tehran• Four killed in Haifa as Israel attacked from three sides• US bases in Kuwait, Saudi Arabia come under attack DUBAI: The US-I...

stories.title: Two people killed in strike on railway bridge in Iranian Kashan — agency
stories.description: Three more people were injured

stories.title: US to continue hostilities in Iran for at least another month — expert
stories.description: Niamh McBurney noted that Washington’s repeated changes to deadlines for reaching a deal do not necessarily signal an approaching de-escalation in the region

stories.title: Israel urges US against agreement with Iran without Strait of Hormuz deal — Axios
stories.description: The portal reported that the leaders of Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates also urged Washington against concessions on the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Brent oil price rises to $111 per barrel amid latest threats against Iran — NYT
stories.description: According to the publication, the price of a barrel of Brent crude oil has risen by 52% since the beginning of the conflict

stories.title: US lost aircraft worth more than $500 million during rescue of pilot in Iran — expert
stories.description: Alexander Stepanov opined that the rescue operation was primarily a public relations effort, driven by the Trump administration’s desire for some form of victory

stories.title: Air raid alert declared in Israel, explosions rock area around Tel Aviv
stories.description: Earlier, the Israeli military said it had detected missile launches from Iran toward the country

stories.title: Iran carries out strikes on several petrochemical facilities in Saudi Arabia
stories.description: According to the media outlet, the attacks targeted Sadra Chemical, ExxonMobil, Dow Chemical and Chevron Phillips facilities

stories.title: Press review: Iran rejects US ultimatum as EU seeks to strip Hungary of voting rights
stories.description: Top stories from the Russian press on Tuesday, April 7th

stories.title: Seventeen residential areas in Iran come under airstrikes this morning — Red Crescent
stories.description: No information on casualties has been reported

stories.title: Pezeshkian places himself among 14 million Iranians ready to die for their country
stories.description: Iran’s Parliament Speaker Mohammad Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf earlier said that some 7 million Iranian citizens are ready defend their country

stories.title: At least 18 civilians killed, 24 wounded in airstrikes on northern Iran
stories.description: Deputy Governor Qodratollah Seif said these actions would not go unanswered

stories.title: IN BRIEF: Russia evacuates personnel from Iran’s Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant
stories.description: The Russian embassy expressed gratitude to the Armenian authorities "for their kind attitude and quick handling of exit procedures" for Rosatom personnel

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
